latestRelease.body:
# ThunderID ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/thunder-id/thunderid.svg)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/thunder-id/thunderid.svg)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/thunder-id/thunderid/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/thunder-id/thunderid?branch=main)
[![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/thunder-id/thunderid?color=blue)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/latest)


ThunderID is a lightweight, open-source Identity and Access Management (IAM) engine built to secure access for humans, AI agents, and machines.

Designed for the agentic era, ThunderID provides a developer-first IAM platform and supporting tools for securing applications, APIs, services, and agent-driven workflows across traditional and decentralized identity ecosystems, with post-quantum-ready security built in from the start.

Core design goals of ThunderID include:
- **Agent-native identity:** Manage AI agents as first-class identities with delegated authority, consent-aware access, traceability, and support for issuing verifiable credentials to agents. ThunderID also aims to expose IAM capabilities through interfaces that agents can use safely and programmatically.
- **Decentralized identity:** Bridge the adoption gap for relying parties by making it practical for service providers to consume, verify, and trust decentralized identity in real-world applications, including DIDs, verifiable credentials, digital wallets, trust registries, and issuer-verifier-holder interaction models.
- **Cloud-native IAM:** Provide a lightweight, containerized identity product that can run across on-premises and cloud environments, with declarative identity flows, policies, and configuration suitable for automation, versioning, and GitOps practices.
- **Post-quantum-safe security:** Build on a crypto-agile foundation where algorithms, key types, signing methods, and token protection mechanisms can evolve over time, including support for post-quantum-safe algorithms and hybrid transition approaches across key management, credential issuance, assertions, and secure service-to-service communication.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.39.0 -->

## What's Changed
### 🚀 Features
* Bring-in JavaScript ecosystem SDKs to ThunderID monorepo by @brionmario in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2735
### ✨ Improvements
* Add Clickable Option for the user when consent is timeout. by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2722
* Migrate pnpm to v11 by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2721
* Migrate public key discovery to RuntimeCryptoProvider by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2657
* Added documentation style updates to Thunder docs. by @himeshsiriwardana in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2743
* Update old SDK references to new `@thunderid/*` namespace by @brionmario in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2762
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Add app name to invite email template data by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2699
* Ignore empty credential values during provisioning by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2669
* Fix credential handling in user onboarding flow request by @thiva-k in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2728
* Remove flow type constraint from OAuth executor by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2734
* Improve declarative resource usertype parsing by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2718


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/compare/v0.38.0...v0.39.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with ThunderID quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run ThunderID

You can run ThunderID either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.39.0 release of ThunderID and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.39.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunderid-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunderid-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunderid-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunderid-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunderid-0.39.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunderid-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunderid-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run ThunderID using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.39.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start ThunderID**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the ThunderID server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the ThunderID Console

Follow these steps to access the ThunderID Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

ThunderID provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.39.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.39.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of ThunderID, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `executionId` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<execution_id>` with the `executionId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"executionId":"<execution_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

# ThunderID ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/thunder-id/thunderid.svg)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/thunder-id/thunderid.svg)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/thunder-id/thunderid/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/thunder-id/thunderid?branch=main)
[![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/thunder-id/thunderid?color=blue)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/latest)


ThunderID is a lightweight, open-source Identity and Access Management (IAM) engine built to secure access for humans, AI agents, and machines.

Designed for the agentic era, ThunderID provides a developer-first IAM platform and supporting tools for securing applications, APIs, services, and agent-driven workflows across traditional and decentralized identity ecosystems, with post-quantum-ready security built in from the start.

Core design goals of ThunderID include:
- **Agent-native identity:** Manage AI agents as first-class identities with delegated authority, consent-aware access, traceability, and support for issuing verifiable credentials to agents. ThunderID also aims to expose IAM capabilities through interfaces that agents can use safely and programmatically.
- **Decentralized identity:** Bridge the adoption gap for relying parties by making it practical for service providers to consume, verify, and trust decentralized identity in real-world applications, including DIDs, verifiable credentials, digital wallets, trust registries, and issuer-verifier-holder interaction models.
- **Cloud-native IAM:** Provide a lightweight, containerized identity product that can run across on-premises and cloud environments, with declarative identity flows, policies, and configuration suitable for automation, versioning, and GitOps practices.
- **Post-quantum-safe security:** Build on a crypto-agile foundation where algorithms, key types, signing methods, and token protection mechanisms can evolve over time, including support for post-quantum-safe algorithms and hybrid transition approaches across key management, credential issuance, assertions, and secure service-to-service communication.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.39.0 -->

## What's Changed
### 🚀 Features
* Bring-in JavaScript ecosystem SDKs to ThunderID monorepo by @brionmario in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2735
### ✨ Improvements
* Add Clickable Option for the user when consent is timeout. by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2722
* Migrate pnpm to v11 by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2721
* Migrate public key discovery to RuntimeCryptoProvider by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2657
* Added documentation style updates to Thunder docs. by @himeshsiriwardana in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2743
* Update old SDK references to new `@thunderid/*` namespace by @brionmario in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2762
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Add app name to invite email template data by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2699
* Ignore empty credential values during provisioning by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2669
* Fix credential handling in user onboarding flow request by @thiva-k in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2728
* Remove flow type constraint from OAuth executor by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2734
* Improve declarative resource usertype parsing by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2718


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/compare/v0.38.0...v0.39.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with ThunderID quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run ThunderID

You can run ThunderID either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.39.0 release of ThunderID and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.39.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunderid-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunderid-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunderid-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunderid-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunderid-0.39.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/thunderid-0.39.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunderid-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunderid-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run ThunderID using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.39.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start ThunderID**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the ThunderID server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the ThunderID Console

Follow these steps to access the ThunderID Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

ThunderID provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.39.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.39.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.39.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.39.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of ThunderID, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `executionId` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<execution_id>` with the `executionId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"executionId":"<execution_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

releases.name: ThunderID v0.39.0

releases.body:
# ThunderID ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/thunder-id/thunderid.svg)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/thunder-id/thunderid.svg)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/thunder-id/thunderid/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/thunder-id/thunderid?branch=main)
[![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/thunder-id/thunderid?color=blue)](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/latest)


ThunderID is a lightweight, open-source Identity and Access Management (IAM) engine built to secure access for humans, AI agents, and machines.

Designed for the agentic era, ThunderID provides a developer-first IAM platform and supporting tools for securing applications, APIs, services, and agent-driven workflows across traditional and decentralized identity ecosystems, with post-quantum-ready security built in from the start.

Core design goals of ThunderID include:
- **Agent-native identity:** Manage AI agents as first-class identities with delegated authority, consent-aware access, traceability, and support for issuing verifiable credentials to agents. ThunderID also aims to expose IAM capabilities through interfaces that agents can use safely and programmatically.
- **Decentralized identity:** Bridge the adoption gap for relying parties by making it practical for service providers to consume, verify, and trust decentralized identity in real-world applications, including DIDs, verifiable credentials, digital wallets, trust registries, and issuer-verifier-holder interaction models.
- **Cloud-native IAM:** Provide a lightweight, containerized identity product that can run across on-premises and cloud environments, with declarative identity flows, policies, and configuration suitable for automation, versioning, and GitOps practices.
- **Post-quantum-safe security:** Build on a crypto-agile foundation where algorithms, key types, signing methods, and token protection mechanisms can evolve over time, including support for post-quantum-safe algorithms and hybrid transition approaches across key management, credential issuance, assertions, and secure service-to-service communication.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.38.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Use `types` instead of `schemas` in user-types list API by @thiva-k in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2622
* Update the product reference to thunderid by @senthalan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2685
* Onboard recovery flow by @NutharaNR in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2533
### ✨ Improvements
* Add external IDP token exchange via JWKS-based verification by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2546
* Align credential schema attribute handling in provisioning by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2553
* Allow provisioning executor to prompt optional credentials by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2636
* Rename inbound client store constants by @thiva-k in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2615
* Route console user creation through flow execution by @Copilot in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2634
* Improve application-agent error handling parity by @thiva-k in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2637
* Use react-router instead of @tanstack/react-router in api based sample by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2668
* Make Encrypt, Decrypt and GetTLSMaterial accept KeyRef pointer in RuntimeCryptoProvider by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2614
* Support running with exported resources by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2653
* Bump @asgardeo/react to 0.24.2 by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2689
* Add createdAt and updatedAt fields to OrganizationUnit API by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2649
* Enforce standard time on CREATED_AT and UPDATED_AT fields in UserDB schemas by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2680
* Fix #2630 - Product name not applying on the illustrations by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2675
* Add react releases page to display Thunder release information and download options by @LinukaAr in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2415
* Refactor role assignments to a separate service by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2646
* Changes from the proof-reading by @sagara-gunathunga in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2706
* Add filter support to OrganizationUnit API  by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2670
* Bump `@asgardeo/react` sdk version by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2714
* Add composite store support for translations by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2711
* Add federated user consistency validation by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2717
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Capitalize Agent in console UI by @thiva-k in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2629
* Fix malformed export yaml of application by @thiva-k in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2696
* Fix OU dropdown handling in ambiguous login flow by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2701
* Allow user disambiguation for more than two OUs by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2702

## New Contributors
* @sagara-gunathunga made their first contribution in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2706
* @sadilchamishka made their first contribution in https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/pull/2703

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/compare/v0.37.0...v0.38.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with ThunderID quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run ThunderID

You can run ThunderID either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.38.0 release of ThunderID and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.38.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunderid-0.38.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/thunderid-0.38.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunderid-0.38.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/thunderid-0.38.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunderid-0.38.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/thunderid-0.38.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunderid-0.38.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/thunderid-0.38.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunderid-0.38.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/thunderid-0.38.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunderid-0.38.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunderid-0.38.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run ThunderID using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.38.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start ThunderID**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the ThunderID server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the ThunderID Console

Follow these steps to access the ThunderID Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

ThunderID provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.38.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/thunder-id/thunderid/releases/download/v0.38.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.38.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.38.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.38.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of ThunderID, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `executionId` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<execution_id>` with the `executionId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"executionId":"<execution_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

releases.body:
# ThunderID ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)
[![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/asgardeo/thunder?color=blue)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/latest)

ThunderID is a lightweight, open-source Identity and Access Management (IAM) engine built to secure access for humans, AI agents, and machines.

Designed for the agentic era, ThunderID provides a developer-first IAM platform and supporting tools for securing applications, APIs, services, and agent-driven workflows across traditional and decentralized identity ecosystems, with post-quantum-ready security built in from the start.

Core design goals of ThunderID include:
- **Agent-native identity:** Manage AI agents as first-class identities with delegated authority, consent-aware access, traceability, and support for issuing verifiable credentials to agents. ThunderID also aims to expose IAM capabilities through interfaces that agents can use safely and programmatically.
- **Decentralized identity:** Bridge the adoption gap for relying parties by making it practical for service providers to consume, verify, and trust decentralized identity in real-world applications, including DIDs, verifiable credentials, digital wallets, trust registries, and issuer-verifier-holder interaction models.
- **Cloud-native IAM:** Provide a lightweight, containerized identity product that can run across on-premises and cloud environments, with declarative identity flows, policies, and configuration suitable for automation, versioning, and GitOps practices.
- **Post-quantum-safe security:** Build on a crypto-agile foundation where algorithms, key types, signing methods, and token protection mechanisms can evolve over time, including support for post-quantum-safe algorithms and hybrid transition approaches across key management, credential issuance, assertions, and secure service-to-service communication.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.37.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Rebrand `Thunder` -> `ThunderID` by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2544
* Migrate user schema to entity type and introduce `/agent-types` API by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2550
* Refactor SQL queries to use double quotes for table names. by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2555
* Add standardized authentication error responses to security middleware by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2582
### 🚀 Features
* Introduce agent entity and APIs by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2503
* Magic Link Implementation by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1879
* ACR values support by @sacrana0 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2203
* Onboard Agent management UI by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2606
### ✨ Improvements
* Add test coverage for import-export feature UIs by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2514
* Add localized client metadata support for applications and DCR by @nandhu-kumar in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2286
* Allow retry of flow step on recoverable error by @NutharaNR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2416
* Add layout positions and missing variants to user onboarding flow by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2525
* Make system resource server handle and identifier configurable by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2487
* Add dynamic input control properties to ProvisioningExecutor by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2518
* Add export support for resource servers and roles by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2535
* Make welcome page to show for any new tab session by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2548
* Implement and migrate runtime signing flows to RuntimeCryptoProvider.Sign by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2479
* Add state parameter to federated OAuth authorize URL by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2545
* Disable auto-inferred registration flow by default by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2549
* Rename export field to user type by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2558
* Re-arrange crypto service structure by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2552
* Refactor welcome screen redirection logic by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2577
* Add executor input configuration UI to flow builder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2570
* Refactor Dependency Injection of Cache and Encryption by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2474
* Export/Improt imporvement: support groups and import roles and resources by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2569
* Improvements to the Magic Link Authentication by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2579
* 1219 | Add JWE encryption support for ID tokens by @nandhu-kumar in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2542
* Revamp Thunder OSS Site Landing by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2586
* Refactor inbound models usage by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2534
* Allow agent login through flows and improve application-agent separation by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2573
* Add support for wildcards in the host component of registered redirect URIs by @RushanNanayakkara in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2600
* Add production deployment guideline documentation by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2596
* Update API Contracts by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2599
* Validate user attributes with user types by @Malith-19 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2588
* Make the encrypt function accept AlgorithmParams pointer by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2611
* Add new Thunder ID theme by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2620
* Refactor agent frontend components by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2613
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Resolve credential attributes from schema in provisioning executor by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2537
* Update agent yaml error codes by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2563
* Add support to customize app favicon of Console & Gate by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2557
* Fix listing tables scrolling issue by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2578
* Update subtitle translation to include product name by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2631

## New Contributors
* @ranuka-laksika made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2528
* @ravindu439 made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2590
* @Malith-19 made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2588

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.36.0...v0.37.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with ThunderID quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run ThunderID

You can run ThunderID either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.37.0 release of ThunderID and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.37.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunderid-0.37.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/thunderid-0.37.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunderid-0.37.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/thunderid-0.37.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunderid-0.37.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/thunderid-0.37.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunderid-0.37.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/thunderid-0.37.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunderid-0.37.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/thunderid-0.37.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunderid-0.37.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunderid-0.37.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run ThunderID using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.37.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start ThunderID**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the ThunderID server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the ThunderID Console

Follow these steps to access the ThunderID Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

ThunderID provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.37.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.37.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.37.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.37.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.37.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of ThunderID, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `executionId` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<execution_id>` with the `executionId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"executionId":"<execution_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```
