releases.name: ThunderID v0.37.0

releases.body:
# Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.36.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Harden CORS origin matching and preflight handling by @RushanNanayakkara in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2494
* Extract OAuth and client configurations from application into a reusable module by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2457
### 🚀 Features
* Pushed authorization request (PAR) support by @sacrana0 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2377
* Add import-export feature UIs + welcome page UI by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2504
### ✨ Improvements
* Route federated authenticators through authn provider by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2397
* Improve drag-and-drop reordering and element insertion in flow builder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2449
* Wildcard in redirection URI support by @anushasunkada in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2437
* Add missing executors to flow builder UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2454
* Authorization Server Issuer Identification (RFC 9207) Support by @SajidMannikeri17 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2387
* Flexible schema support with default fallback in Email Executor by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2461
* JWE encrypt and decrypt migration to use RuntimeCryptoProvider by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2455
* Add flow template layouts, validation, and auto-assign connections by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2476
* add JWE and nested JWT support for UserInfo endpoint by @nandhu-kumar in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2394
* Add import endpoint support by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2427
* JWE encrypt and decrypt alg support to use RuntimeCryptoProvider by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2491
* Email Executor generic template data handling by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2485
* Refactor to fix dependency injection gaps by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2460
* Drive provisioning inputs from user schema by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2450
* fix(userinfo): Align responseType handling and content-type with OIDC spec by @nandhu-kumar in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2498
* Improve hardening cors origin matching and preflight handling by @RushanNanayakkara in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2507
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Add missing i18n translations for user type selector in self signup by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2468
* Fix flaky ssrf test by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2486
* Enable `refresh token` in grant type in Console by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2496
* Improve application export: omit client secret for public clients, emit env values with JSON array support by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2500
* Remove verified boolean attributes from default Person schema by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2510
* Fix YAML quoting of flow export values to prevent import failures by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2511

## New Contributors
* @anushasunkada made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2437
* @SajidMannikeri17 made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2387

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.35.0...v0.36.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run Thunder

You can run Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.36.0 release of Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.36.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.36.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/thunder-0.36.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.36.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/thunder-0.36.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.36.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/thunder-0.36.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.36.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/thunder-0.36.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.36.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/thunder-0.36.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.36.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.36.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.36.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.36.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.36.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.36.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.36.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.36.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `executionId` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<execution_id>` with the `executionId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"executionId":"<execution_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

breaking: Harden CORS origin matching and preflight handling
Extract OAuth and client configurations from application into a reusable module
bugs: Add missing i18n translations for user type selector in self signup
Fix flaky ssrf test
Enable `refresh token` in grant type in Console
Improve application export: omit client secret for public clients, emit env values with JSON array support
Remove verified boolean attributes from default Person schema
Fix YAML quoting of flow export values to prevent import failures
features: Pushed authorization request (PAR) support
Add import-export feature UIs + welcome page UI
improvements: Route federated authenticators through authn provider
Improve drag-and-drop reordering and element insertion in flow builder
Wildcard in redirection URI support
Add missing executors to flow builder UI
Authorization Server Issuer Identification (RFC 9207) Support
Flexible schema support with default fallback in Email Executor
JWE encrypt and decrypt migration to use RuntimeCryptoProvider
Add flow template layouts, validation, and auto-assign connections
add JWE and nested JWT support for UserInfo endpoint
Add import endpoint support
JWE encrypt and decrypt alg support to use RuntimeCryptoProvider
Email Executor generic template data handling
Refactor to fix dependency injection gaps
Drive provisioning inputs from user schema
fix(userinfo): Align responseType handling and content-type with OIDC spec
Improve hardening cors origin matching and preflight handling

releases.name: Thunder v0.36.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.35.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Remove NONE app certificate type by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2362
* Support mandatory per step challenge token for flow execution by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2369
* Refactor error handling to use I18nServiceError for improved internationalization support by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2371
* Complete i18n migration of the service error layer by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2396
### 🚀 Features
* Introduce ConfigCryptoProvider Interface and adapt it's usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2364
* Implement RuntimeCryptoProvider with Encrypt and Decrypt functions by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2403
* Implement RFC 8707 Resource Indicators for OAuth 2.0 by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2343
### ✨ Improvements
* Implement authentication provider manager by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2316
* Refactor in-memory cache implementation to improve performance by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2326
* Unify UUID copy UIs + Add Delete capability to Applications edit page's Danger Zone by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2344
* Add SMS-based self-invite registration flow by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2305
* Encrypt flow context full object by @NutharaNR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2331
* Remove encryption/decryption from un/marshalling of AuthUser by @NutharaNR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2373
* Include OU details in httpRequestExecutor by @NutharaNR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2376
* Improve flow builder UX and design by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2384
* Add support to render a Custom component during the flow execution by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2385
* Split monolithic FlowBuilderCoreContext into 4 domain-specific contexts by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2390
* Centralize flow builder validation into declarative computed model by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2393
* Add system attributes search in IdentifyEntity fallback by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2304
* Extract VariantSelect component and replace window CustomEvents with FlowEventsContext by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2406
* Update entity store constants order by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2417
* Unify user and app member types to internal entity type by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2303
* Show organization unit handle in role responses and edit page by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2401
* Add redis and DB retry on failure support by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2367
* Replace PluginRegistry singleton with React Context-based FlowPluginProvider by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2433
* Introduce runtime flow segmentation by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2431
* Implement cache-backed entity store for improved performance in entity lookups by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2422
* Add env variables to export request by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2398
* Refactor ApiError interface to support i18n error format by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2446
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix the default login screen (with slogan) intermittently appearing in business app logins by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2328
* Email Executor No Operation, handling and Admin invite Improvements by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2294
* Fix prompt node to skip already satisfied inputs in meta by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2388
* Handle runtime SMTP errors gracefully in email executor by @ThaminduR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2068
* Hide edit button for user types with only credential fields by @ThaminduR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2004
* Remove NONE cert in windows bootstrap by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2414
* Removes the redundant Thunder text in navbar by @LinukaAr in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2420
* Mask user IDs in all log output to prevent PII leakage by @ThaminduR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2041
* Improve OAuth app validations by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2434
* Fix challenge token validation for passkey registration by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2444
* Initiate new sign-in from SignUpBox component by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2447


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.34.0...v0.35.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.35.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.35.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.35.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/thunder-0.35.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.35.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/thunder-0.35.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.35.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/thunder-0.35.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.35.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/thunder-0.35.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.35.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/thunder-0.35.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.35.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.35.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.35.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.35.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.35.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.35.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.35.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.35.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `executionId` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<execution_id>` with the `executionId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"executionId":"<execution_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

breaking: Remove NONE app certificate type
Support mandatory per step challenge token for flow execution
Refactor error handling to use I18nServiceError for improved internationalization support
Complete i18n migration of the service error layer
bugs: Fix the default login screen (with slogan) intermittently appearing in business app logins
features: Introduce ConfigCryptoProvider Interface and adapt it's usage
Implement RuntimeCryptoProvider with Encrypt and Decrypt functions
Implement RFC 8707 Resource Indicators for OAuth 2.0
improvements: Implement authentication provider manager
Refactor in-memory cache implementation to improve performance
Unify UUID copy UIs + Add Delete capability to Applications edit page's Danger Zone
Add SMS-based self-invite registration flow
Encrypt flow context full object
Remove encryption/decryption from un/marshalling of AuthUser
Include OU details in httpRequestExecutor
Improve flow builder UX and design
Add support to render a Custom component during the flow execution
Split monolithic FlowBuilderCoreContext into 4 domain-specific contexts
Centralize flow builder validation into declarative computed model
Add system attributes search in IdentifyEntity fallback
Extract VariantSelect component and replace window CustomEvents with FlowEventsContext
Update entity store constants order
Unify user and app member types to internal entity type
Show organization unit handle in role responses and edit page
Add redis and DB retry on failure support
Replace PluginRegistry singleton with React Context-based FlowPluginProvider
Introduce runtime flow segmentation
Implement cache-backed entity store for improved performance in entity lookups
Add env variables to export request
Refactor ApiError interface to support i18n error format
Fix prompt node to skip already satisfied inputs in meta
Handle runtime SMTP errors gracefully in email executor
Hide edit button for user types with only credential fields
Remove NONE cert in windows bootstrap
Removes the redundant Thunder text in navbar
Mask user IDs in all log output to prevent PII leakage
Improve OAuth app validations
Fix challenge token validation for passkey registration
Initiate new sign-in from SignUpBox component

releases.name: Thunder v0.35.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.34.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Rename flowID in flow execution to executionId by @NutharaNR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2213
* Runtime DB in redis by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2218
### ✨ Improvements
* Add script and CI workflow to auto-generate Postman collections from OpenAPI specs by @Kavirubc in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1873
* Add PS256 (RSA-PSS) algorithm support for JWT signing by @BimsaraBodaragama in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2308
* Set required flag to true by default for flow builder input elements by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2324
* Add configurable parentOuId property for OUExecutor by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2322
* Fix custom branding CSS overwritten on color scheme toggle by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2297
* Add SMS invite support to user onboarding flow by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2300
* Fix generated flow graphs crashing in flow builder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2327
* Fix decrypt to use payload KID by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2320
* Replace user provider with entity provider by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2273
* Fix id_token_signing_alg_values_supported to reflect configured signing keys dynamically by @BimsaraBodaragama in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2312
* Support generic OIDC providers as trusted issuer for Thunder instance by @RushanNanayakkara in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2314
* Add integration tests covering oauth2 authorize call when the application is declarative by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2299
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Change make build order by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2301
* Fix missing app logo and sign up URL ordering in generated flows by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2309
* Fix mismatches in Application to entity transformation by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2341

## New Contributors
* @Kavirubc made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1873
* @NutharaNR made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2213
* @BimsaraBodaragama made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2308

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.33.0...v0.34.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.34.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.34.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.34.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/thunder-0.34.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.34.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/thunder-0.34.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.34.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/thunder-0.34.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.34.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/thunder-0.34.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.34.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/thunder-0.34.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.34.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.34.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.34.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.34.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.34.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.34.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.34.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.34.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `executionId` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<execution_id>` with the `executionId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"executionId":"<execution_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"executionId":"<execution_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.
