releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.33.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Add flow native render support for invite flows by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2276
* Add app RBAC support for client credentials flow and generalize RBAC for entities  by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2272
### 🚀 Features
* Secure Thunder with an external identity provider by @RushanNanayakkara in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2270
### ✨ Improvements
* Fix invite wizard centering and hide disambiguation flow by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2266
* Update contribution guide with build, run, debugging, and coding guidelines by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2267
* Add Self Sign Up widget to onboarding-generated auth flows by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2250
* Fix sms-otp hardcoded phone attribute by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2259
* Update axios override to `v1.15.0` by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2232
* Added flows and deployment guides along with fixes for broken links by @himeshsiriwardana in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2177
* Update helm charts to the sub chart architecture by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2285
* Fix token settings crash, validation bypass, and attribute save failure by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2296
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix oauthApp resolve in declarative resources by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2280
* Remove unused observability service dependency from flow executors by @Copilot in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2268
* Refactor error handling for user provisioning in OAuthExecutor by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2282
* Remove unnecessary JSON stringification due to sdk bump by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2290

## New Contributors
* @RushanNanayakkara made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2270

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.32.0...v0.33.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.33.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.33.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.33.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/thunder-0.33.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.33.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/thunder-0.33.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.33.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/thunder-0.33.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.33.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/thunder-0.33.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.33.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/thunder-0.33.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.33.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.33.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.33.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.33.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.33.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.33.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.33.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.33.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

breaking: Add flow native render support for invite flows
Add app RBAC support for client credentials flow and generalize RBAC for entities
bugs: Fix oauthApp resolve in declarative resources
Remove unused observability service dependency from flow executors
Refactor error handling for user provisioning in OAuthExecutor
Remove unnecessary JSON stringification due to sdk bump
features: Secure Thunder with an external identity provider
improvements: Fix invite wizard centering and hide disambiguation flow
Update contribution guide with build, run, debugging, and coding guidelines
Add Self Sign Up widget to onboarding-generated auth flows
Fix sms-otp hardcoded phone attribute
Update axios override to `v1.15.0`
Added flows and deployment guides along with fixes for broken links
Update helm charts to the sub chart architecture
Fix token settings crash, validation bypass, and attribute save failure

releases.name: Thunder v0.33.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.32.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Refactor runtime DB usages to fit redis by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2120
* Onboard entity core and migrate users and applications to entity model by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2104
### 🚀 Features
* Add generic SMS executor for flow-based SMS sending by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2128
* Add runtime user disambiguation for identifying executor by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2225
* Add SMS template support for OTP and flow-based SMS sending by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2217
### ✨ Improvements
* Improve Redis Cache key prefix for Deployment Isolation by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2174
* Add skill to navigate through console by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2207
* Fix flow builder UX issues by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2212
* Improve prompt node with display only mode and action type by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2224
* Add SELECT element and IdentifyingExecutor mode selector to flow builder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2231
* Add client's OU by default in m2m token by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2238
* Add multi-OU user disambiguation for federated authentication flows by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2246
* Show OU picker when single root OU has child OUs by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2255
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Update default issuer helm value by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2187
* Fix image `alt` not resolving `i18n` values in Thunder Gate by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2234
* Remove client prefix from m2m token ou claims by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2243
* Add missing runtime keys to non user attribute list by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2245
* Remove UUID validation from flow/meta endpoint by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2251
* Change vite dependency to rolldown-vite@7.1.14 in pnpm workspace by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2261


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.31.0...v0.32.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.32.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.32.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.32.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/thunder-0.32.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.32.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/thunder-0.32.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.32.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/thunder-0.32.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.32.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/thunder-0.32.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.32.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/thunder-0.32.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.32.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.32.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.32.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.32.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.32.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.32.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.32.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.32.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

releases.name: Thunder v0.32.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.31.0 -->

## What's Changed
### 🚀 Features
* Add Self Registration Invite Flow by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1970
### ✨ Improvements
* Add logoUrl field to organization units and update related resources by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2005
* Add integration tests for declarative resources by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1872
* Refactor application service by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1956
* Add font design support for gate preview by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2052
* Add type filter for role assingment API by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2026
* Add Redis username configuration by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2100
* Provide support for Role UI by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1938
* Make listing UI actions consistent by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2149
* Refactor user schema and group service interfaces to include 'includeDisplay' parameter by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2134
* Provide support to return the roles with tokens by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2137
* Add support for organization unit selection in user type resolution in user invite flow by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2147
* Add 'include=display' support for thunder-console by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2138
* Add display names to bootstrap schema properties by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2178
* Update default flows to show signup link on sign in page by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2181
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix UI inconsistency issues in Listing & other UIs in Thunder Console by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2069
* Fix application logo resolving in default flows by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2072
* Update postman collection for latest apis by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2071
* Fix AuthPageLayout background fallback and CI merge queue skips by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2126
* Hide credential attributes from token attribute selection UI by @ThaminduR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2003
* Update navigation path in `Create Application` button inside Thunder Home by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2153
* Remove registration flow info box and role edit handle field by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/2180

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.30.0...v0.31.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.31.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.31.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.31.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/thunder-0.31.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.31.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/thunder-0.31.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.31.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/thunder-0.31.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.31.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/thunder-0.31.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.31.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/thunder-0.31.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.31.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.31.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.31.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.31.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.31.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.31.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.31.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.31.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.30.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Add argon2id support by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1599
* Move API request body attributes to camelCase by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1897
* Add `clientId` to context runtime data and standardize `userId` usage by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1984
### 🚀 Features
* Implement Redis cache support by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1880
* Add custom CSS builder UI with iframe preview by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1949
### ✨ Improvements
* Add include=display query param int GET users/\{userId\} endpoint by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1885
* Pack consent server with thunder distribution by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1934
* Improve Application `Token` & `Advanced` sections by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1942
* Add OU selection backend support for invited users by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1962
* Improve `Theme` Thumbnail in Thunder Design by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1975
* Add OU selection UI for user invite wizard by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1963
* Add consent documentation by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1982
* Update OC deployment artifacts to support latest OC version by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1953
* Add isReadOnly attribute for list response by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1870
* Support mounting declarative resources via helm by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1994
* Add allowed user schema support to node poperty  by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1996
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Validate application flow types during creation by @Dilusha-Madushan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1906
* Update asgardeo react version to 0.19.0 by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1961
* Fix missing clientId in Windows bootstrap for Console app by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1990
* Add description to ApplicationMetadata in flow/meta response by @ThaminduR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1983
* Fix schema validation failing on user update when credentials are absent by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1995
* Refactor graph cache to use in-memory cache implementation and enhance logging by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1997

## New Contributors
* @Dilusha-Madushan made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1906
* @ThaminduR made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1983

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.29.0...v0.30.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.30.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.30.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/thunder-0.30.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.30.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/thunder-0.30.0-macos-x64.zip) |

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.30.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.30.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.30.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.30.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.30.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

breaking: Add argon2id support
Move API request body attributes to camelCase
Add `clientId` to context runtime data and standardize `userId` usage
bugs: Validate application flow types during creation
Update asgardeo react version to 0.19.0
Fix missing clientId in Windows bootstrap for Console app
Add description to ApplicationMetadata in flow/meta response
Fix schema validation failing on user update when credentials are absent
Refactor graph cache to use in-memory cache implementation and enhance logging
features: Implement Redis cache support
Add custom CSS builder UI with iframe preview
improvements: Add include=display query param int GET users/\{userId\} endpoint
Pack consent server with thunder distribution
Improve Application `Token` & `Advanced` sections
Add OU selection backend support for invited users
Improve `Theme` Thumbnail in Thunder Design
Add OU selection UI for user invite wizard
Add consent documentation
Update OC deployment artifacts to support latest OC version
Add isReadOnly attribute for list response
Support mounting declarative resources via helm
Add allowed user schema support to node poperty

releases.name: Thunder v0.30.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.29.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Standardize OU reference field name and rename OrganizationUnitID to OUID by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1840
### 🚀 Features
* Introduce a Emoji Picker for Resource Logos by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1838
* Email Template & Scenario Type Support for Email Executor by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1831
### ✨ Improvements
* Remove usage of context.TODO  by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1830
* Resolve errors being masked with transactioner by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1866
* Bump `@asgardeo/react` to 0.15.2 and remove redundant `AcceptInvite` validation by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1869
* Minor improvements in composite stores by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1725
* Use attribute cache for generating tokens and userinfo response by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1853
* Improve application validation logic by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1798
* Improve consent form with toggles by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1900
* Add custom CSS capability to Gate App by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1898
* Remove database dependency from transections by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1871
* Add a unique `handle` in Theme and Layout schema by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1901
* Add initial quickstarts and guides to Thunder by @himeshsiriwardana in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1911
* feat(docs): add i18n localization support infrastructure by @littleKitchen in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1254
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix consent executor to include special attributes by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1844
* Fixes white labeling Issues in Thunder Gate by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1875
* Fix refresh issues in `Thunder Gate`  by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1894
* Fix composite get and uuid validation by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1883
* Fix `Invite` flows to support `i18n` translations via Thunder Console by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1910
* Remove logo from accept invite by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1915

## New Contributors
* @littleKitchen made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1254

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.28.0...v0.29.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.29.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.29.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.29.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/thunder-0.29.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.29.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/thunder-0.29.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.29.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/thunder-0.29.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.29.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/thunder-0.29.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.29.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/thunder-0.29.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.29.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.29.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.29.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.29.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.29.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.29.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.29.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.29.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.
