releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.28.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ✨ Improvements
* Process essential attributes based on oidc claims parameter by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1808
* Add credential support and disable unique when credential is checked by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1818
* Create administrators group in boostrap by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1832
* Rename app: thunder-develop -> thunder-console by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1802
* Add support to get unique schema attributes by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1845
* Clear user inputs in prompt nodes for failures by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1839
* Add unique attribute validation for invite flow by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1848
* Remove phone number in the user invite flow by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1849
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Improve Application Settings `Contacts` field & Fix Home page cards responsive issues by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1805
* Disable local cache in helm setup by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1827
* Fix scopes not returned for group-assigned roles by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1815


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.27.0...v0.28.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.28.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.28.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.28.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/thunder-0.28.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.28.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/thunder-0.28.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.28.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/thunder-0.28.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.28.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/thunder-0.28.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.28.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/thunder-0.28.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.28.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.28.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.28.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Thunder Console

Follow these steps to access the Thunder Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/console](https://localhost:8090/console).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.28.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.28.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.28.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.28.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.28.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.27.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Add attribute caching layer by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1753
* Unify name attributes to OIDC standard (given_name/family_name) by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1742
* Introduce EXPIRY_TIME column to FLOW_CONTEXT table by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1731
* Remove enabled flag from application login consent config by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1771
* Refactor SQL indexes for improved lookup efficiency and consistency by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1706
### 🚀 Features
* Integrate consent login page UIs to gate by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1737
* Introduce a `Theme Builder` by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1729
* Add consent components to flow builder UI by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1745
* Home Page for Thunder Develop by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1789
### ✨ Improvements
* Support TLS Termination in Gateway API via Helm Chart using Envoy Gateway by @DharshanSR in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1481
* Application service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1673
* Add `OUResolverExecutor` by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1763
* Remove redundant client credentials grant validation by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1747
* Improve OAuth package error handling by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1716
* Add database cleanup  procedure for expired data by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1760
* Add include=display support to OU user listing endpoints by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1768
* Add display name and avatar support to OU UIs by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1784
* Add optional displayName field to user schema property definitions by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1782
* oauth service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1754
* Display name UI for user type attributes by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1787
* Integration of User Onboarding flow with Email Mechanism by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1748
* Flow service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1765
* Add Runtime Database Cleanup Script by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1734
* Bump oxygen-ui version to v0.8.2 which have fixes for app shell scrolling issues by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1796
* Add credential support and property management to user type edit page by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1799
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Initialize transactioner regardless the DB mode by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1762
* Fix consent filtering for the default client by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1773
* Add `Sign Up URL Widget` to Flows & remove the static sign up url in `Thunder Gate` by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1785
* Fix Application URL update issues in `Edit` view by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1794
* Remove secret regenerate component for public client in develop app by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1800


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.26.0...v0.27.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

Follow these steps to download the 0.27.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.27.0 release**

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.27.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.27.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.27.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.27.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.27.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.27.0-macos-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.27.0-<os>-<arch>/

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json

        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"

    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash

    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.27.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.27.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.27.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.27.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.27.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.27.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.27.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.27.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2026 WSO2 LLC.

Remove enabled flag from application login consent config

bugs: Initialize transactioner regardless the DB mode
Fix consent filtering for the default client
Add `Sign Up URL Widget` to Flows & remove the static sign up url in `Thunder Gate`

Remove secret regenerate component for public client in develop app

Home Page for Thunder Develop

Add `OUResolverExecutor`
Remove redundant client credentials grant validation

Add include=display support to OU user listing endpoints
Add display name and avatar support to OU UIs

oauth service context propagation and transection usage
Display name UI for user type attributes
Integration of User Onboarding flow with Email Mechanism

Add Runtime Database Cleanup Script
Bump oxygen-ui version to v0.8.2 which have fixes for app shell scrolling issues

releases.name: Thunder v0.27.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.26.0 -->

## What's Changed
### 🚀 Features
* Add core email sending capability with system config and SMTP client by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1655
### ✨ Improvements
* Add cache-backed user schema store and display attribute methods by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1679
* Introduce `Stack` component to flows by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1702
* [Docs] Introduce `use cases` section for the documentation with B2C, B2B, and AI agent use cases by @AnuradhaSK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1703
* Add batch fetch support for users and groups and fix display attribute resolution by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1690
* Refactor invite executor to support send/verify modes by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1719
* Add UI for showing display attribute of users by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1735
* Add include=display support for user and group listing endpoints by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1710
* Define display attribute in default user schemas by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1740
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix GitHub edit URL in documentation by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1722
* Fix declarative resource validations by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1720

## New Contributors
* @AnuradhaSK made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1703

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.25.0...v0.26.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.26.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.26.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.26.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/thunder-0.26.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.26.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/thunder-0.26.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.26.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/thunder-0.26.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.26.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/thunder-0.26.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.26.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/thunder-0.26.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.26.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.26.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.26.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.26.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.26.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.26.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.26.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.26.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)
[![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/commits/main)
[![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/asgardeo/thunder.svg)](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/issues)
[![codecov.io](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder/coverage.svg?branch=main)](https://codecov.io/github/asgardeo/thunder?branch=main)

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.25.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Add support to persist authenticated user token and available attributes in flow context by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1579
* Rename UUID-based primary key columns to ID by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1605
* Refactoring thunderdb/identitydb to configdb by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1678
* Remove plain method support in pkce by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1586
### 🚀 Features
* Introduce Translations UI by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1626
* Add systemAttributes support to user schema by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1657
* Add consent mgt service with attribute config integration by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1619
* Add Client Secret regeneration functionality and related components by @kavindadimuthu in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1669
* Integrate system authz for group package by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1677
* feature: private_key_jwt client authentication by @sacrana0 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1555
* Add consent enforcer service and consent executor by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1697
### ✨ Improvements
* Add Windows prerequisites for SQLite3 and PowerShell 7 by @yehenidodanwela in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1502
* Migrate user types API hooks to TanStack Query by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1606
* Return initialize errors to servicemanager by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1567
* 1551 | Add nonce support for OIDC authorization code flow by @nandhu-kumar in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1604
* Refactor OU hierarchy resolver to decouple self-inclusion logic by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1649
* Add userinfo to discovery endpoints by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1642
* Update auth code generation mechanism by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1643
* Make basic auth scheme comparison case-insensitive.  by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1636
* Add strict attribute validation in user schema by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1630
* Refactor exporters structs by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1650
* Update `aud` in introspect to support array by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1658
* Add URL decode for basic auth client credentials by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1634
* Add configuration for switching userprovider implementation by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1639
* Improve authz code consumption logic by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1632
* Add organization unit selection step to user creation wizard by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1666
* Add strict display attribute validation with dot-notation support in user schema by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1664
* Improve thunder_integrate_react_sdk for app native login by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1676
* [Docs] Enabling Docusaurus versions by @indeewari in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1312
* OU service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1651
* Add front-end email validation for user form by @garuka-satharasinghe in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1623
* Add `prompt` parameter support in authorize request by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1656
* Add `WWW-Authenticate` for client auth errors by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1660
* Add authentication for token introspect endpoint by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1686
* Use ouId from token as fallback during user creation by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1685
* Add HTTP headers to prevent frame embedding authz requests by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1681
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Add handling for THUNDER_SKIP_SECURITY environment variable in setup script by @yehenidodanwela in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1554
* Change refresh grant error for additional scopes by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1641
* Update inconsistent OAuth error codes by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1637
* Fix: Replace xxd with openssl in build.sh by @RandithaK in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1611
* Update token exchange error code by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1628
* Remove CORS from authz request by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1659
* Fix incorrect refactoring from thunderdb to configdb in .github/workflows/release-builder.yml by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1684
* Update ROLE_PERMISSION.PERMISSION column Size by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1695
* Fix OIDC scope filtering in flow initialization by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1700

## New Contributors
* @yehenidodanwela made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1554
* @RandithaK made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1611
* @indeewari made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1312
* @garuka-satharasinghe made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1623
* @kavindadimuthu made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1669

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.24.0...v0.25.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.25.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.25.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.25.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/thunder-0.25.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.25.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/thunder-0.25.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.25.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/thunder-0.25.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.25.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/thunder-0.25.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.25.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/thunder-0.25.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.25.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.25.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.25.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.25.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.25.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.25.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.25.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.25.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

releases.name: Thunder v0.25.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.24.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Refactor resource package to remove int based FK reference by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1557
* Implement Schema-driven credential attributes logic by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1552
* Remove INT ID column in DB Schema by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1547
* Refactor flow/mgt package to remove int based FK reference by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1584
### 🚀 Features
* Implement Schema-driven credential attributes UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1560
* Integrate system authz for OU package by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1534
* Integrate system authz for user package by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1575
* Integrate system authz for user schema package by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1602
### ✨ Improvements
* fixed: unit testcase failure in observability package by @sacrana0 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1423
* Add pagination support for Organization Unit tree picker by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1492
* Update Oxygen UI version to 0.7.0 release along with few bug fixes and test coverage improvements by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1465
* Update list table to oxygen UI ListingTable.DataGrid.Card component by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1505
* Onboard system authorization core by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1500
* Add edit/view icons to Applications, Users, UserTypes listings by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1529
* Notification service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1535
* IDP service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1548
* Improve thunder docs landing page by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1470
* Move transaction initialization logic to init.go by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1541
* Support configurable OAuth UserInfo response_type (JSON/JWS) with JWS signing implementation by @nandhu-kumar in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1362
* Support storing application metadata and passing metadata to authn provider by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1559
* Improve OAuth handler-service layer separation by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1509
* Add credential input field for user creation wizard by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1572
* Support composite store for roles by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1532
* Update the default resource limits and requests in helm chart by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1573
* Resolve context conflicts in nodecontext and enginecontext by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1571
* Improve styling in thunder-docs landing pages by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1576
* Composite store support for IdP by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1401
* Support composite store for resource service by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1530
* Add composite store support for user schemas by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1570
* Add access token validator for userinfo by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1590
* Onboard Action wise system authz policy by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1577
* Add ID token in refresh token grant flow by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1585
* Add composite store support for themes and layouts by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1600
* Define structure for requested attributes by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1574
* Add composite store support for users by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1607
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Support registration in authz executor by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1145
* Fix authnprovider dependency in auth assert executor by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1549
* Add meta for default-basic-passkey-flow by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1533
* Add missing attributes in application yaml mapping by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1563
* Add `at+jwt` in access token header by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1588

## New Contributors
* @DharshanSR made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1531
* @nandhu-kumar made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1362

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.23.0...v0.24.0

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.24.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.24.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.24.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.24.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.24.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
