releases.name: Thunder v0.24.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.23.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Refactor application DB schema by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1444
* Add support for external IDPs in user invite flows by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1448
* Add user core pluggability by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1420
* Remove application-level issuer configs by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1464
* Add authorization for DCR endpoint by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1486
### 🚀 Features
* Introduce an aggregated flow metadata endpoint by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1410
* Add group management UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1483
### ✨ Improvements
* Resource service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1402
* [DOC] Add Passkey Docs by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1333
* Ignore generated release page from commits by @himeshsiriwardana in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1408
* Improve declarative resources in OU by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1419
* Add Gateway API HTTPRoute support in helm chart by @isala404 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1403
* Update Go version to 1.26 by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1437
* Update golang base image by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1433
* Composite store support for Flows by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1404
* Add onIncomplete support for task execution nodes by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1454
* Improve skip security middleware handling by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1453
* Add group member add/remove endpoints and other improvements to /groups endpoint by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1434
* Add composite store support for applications by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1396
* Add onIncomplete edge support to the flow builder by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1468
* Userschema service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1436
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Improve client secret update logic by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1415
* Add scope_claims, Scopes, and UserInfo to declartive resource parsing by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1449
* Allow retrying authentication with basic auth errors by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1477
* Fix user credential update through PUT endpoint by @nishagii in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1432

## New Contributors
* @sacrana0 made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1413
* @isala404 made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1403
* @nishagii made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1432

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.22.0...v0.23.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.23.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.23.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.23.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/thunder-0.23.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.23.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/thunder-0.23.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.23.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/thunder-0.23.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.23.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/thunder-0.23.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.23.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/thunder-0.23.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.23.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.23.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.23.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.23.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.23.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash

    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.23.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.23.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.23.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.22.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Update auth assertion callback URL by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1311
* Add support for configuring separate attributes for OIDC userinfo by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1309
* Introduce a new `/design` API to replace `/branding` API by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1326
### ✨ Improvements
* Add MCP authorization  by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1184
* Disable registration flow in Develop App by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1330
* Reorganize JWT/JWE into JOSE package structure by @Copilot in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1307
* Added copilot instructions for documentation and vale rules for style checks by @himeshsiriwardana in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1303
* Update Passkey Authentication Atomic APIs by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1346
* Add Passkey as an authentication option and dynamically construct the authentication flow graphs at Application Creation by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1348
* Add user info config view to application edit by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1337
* Add claims locales parameter support in authorize request by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1336
* Group service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1344
* Implement Organization Unit Tree UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1350
* Remove Session Cleanup of WebAuthn Session Data Table by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1372
* Add claims support to OIDC discovery by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1328
* Add offset limit support to composite store by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1305
* Added a style guide for human authors and improved agent instructions by @himeshsiriwardana in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1379
* Make appId, idpId, senderId available for flow context by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1380
* Improve handling credential inputs in authentication flows by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1384
* Cert service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1360
* Add declarative resource support for themes and layouts by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1381
* Refactor MCP package by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1352
* Add support to store a Logo URL & Design Preferences (Theme / Layout) per OU by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1383
* Introduce a `Releases` page in Thunder documentation by @LinukaAr in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1387
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Stop browsers from caching `index.html` files by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1324
* Fix incorrect registration flow inference for passkey on-the-fly registration flows by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1331
* Add openid scope validation for userinfo endpoint by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1371
* Fix on the fly passkey registration ending up in infinite loops because of unnecessary onFailure options. by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1375
* Fix unique attribute conflict with same user when updating by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1382
* Fix child OU save bug by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1388
* Add foreign key pragma configs for sqlite by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1392
* Add registration graph for default-basic-passkey-flow  by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1395

## New Contributors
* @LinukaAr made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1387

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.21.0...v0.22.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.22.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.22.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.22.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/thunder-0.22.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.22.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/thunder-0.22.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.22.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/thunder-0.22.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.22.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/thunder-0.22.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.22.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/thunder-0.22.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.22.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.22.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.22.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.22.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.22.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.22.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.22.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.22.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.21.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Rename application root level token config to `assertion` by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1266
### 🚀 Features
* Implement JWE service by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1267
* Username-less passkey authentication by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1268
### ✨ Improvements
* Add unit tests for thunder-gate app by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1255
* refactor: migrate from UUIDv4 to UUIDv7 across all resources by @priyanshoon in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1235
* Add `required_attributes` support for OAuth and auth assertion executor by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1258
* Unify passkey authentication and registration flow graph handles by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1287
* Role service context propagation and transection usage by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1279
* Generate separate key pair for digital signatures by @Copilot in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1131
* React Vanilla Sample App Improvements for Passkey by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1293
* Introduce `claims` support for OAuth flows by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1288
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix `run` command issues on Windows Platforms by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1302
* Add an option to copy the client secret of confidential apps by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1323

## New Contributors
* @priyanshoon made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1235

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.20.0...v0.21.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.21.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.21.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.21.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/thunder-0.21.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.21.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/thunder-0.21.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.21.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/thunder-0.21.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.21.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/thunder-0.21.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.21.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/thunder-0.21.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.21.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.21.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.16.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.21.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.21.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.21.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.21.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.21.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.

breaking: Rename application root level token config to `assertion`
bugs: Fix `run` command issues on Windows Platforms
Add an option to copy the client secret of confidential apps
features: Implement JWE service
Username-less passkey authentication
improvements: Add unit tests for thunder-gate app
refactor: migrate from UUIDv4 to UUIDv7 across all resources
Add `required_attributes` support for OAuth and auth assertion executor
Unify passkey authentication and registration flow graph handles
Role service context propagation and transection usage
Generate separate key pair for digital signatures
Introduce `claims` support for OAuth flows

releases.name: Thunder v0.21.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.20.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Rename from immutable resources to declarative resources by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1182
* Improve Passkey Authentication by Enabling Attribute-Based User Identification by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1203
* Improve default attributes handling in flows and oauth by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1248
### 🚀 Features
* Add passkey support for the React Vanilla Sample App by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1096
* Introduce Thunder Documentation by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1188
* Implement OU management UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1215
### ✨ Improvements
* Add passkey template and components to the login flow builder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1173
* Onboard i18n support to flow builder UI components and improve test coverage in thunder-develop app by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1175
* Add step-up auth via SMS OTP to react-api-based-sample by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1186
* Bump SDK version for react-sdk-sample app by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1194
* Improve UI support for passkey executor in react-vanilla-sample app by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1195
* Bump to @asgardeo/react v0.9.2 with auto-login after signup support by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1190
* Add passkey registration for login flow templates and improvements to flow-builder UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1213
* Optimize user store calls in BasicAuth and SMSAuth executors by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1201
* Refactor user service to use transactions by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1205
* Add shared testing utilities for Thunder applications by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1244
* Improve prompt nodes to auto select single actions by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1247
* Support k8s secrets for db passwords by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1245
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix bootstrap scripts to fetch OUs by handle instead of picking first from list by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1169
* Fix auto-login after self-registration by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1170
* Add leeway for time claims in token validation by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1189
* Fix the ID Token Validation issues due to invalid Issuer configured in the SDK by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1232


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.19.0...v0.20.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.20.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.20.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.20.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/thunder-0.20.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.20.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/thunder-0.20.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.20.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/thunder-0.20.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.20.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/thunder-0.20.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.20.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/thunder-0.20.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.20.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.20.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.16.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.20.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.20.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.20.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.20.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.20.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.19.0 -->

## What's Changed
### 🚀 Features
* Introduce initial MCP setup with application and flow tools by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1122
* Add admin-initiated user registration flow support by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1042
* Onboard application editing capabilities by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1150
* Introduce User Invite feature by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1147
* Add passkey executor support for flow execution by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1104
### ✨ Improvements
* Add react-api-based-sample app to the release artifacts by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1101
* Allow defining meta for TASK_EXECUTION nodes by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1105
* Bump golang.org/x/crypto from 0.44.0 to 0.45.0 in /backend by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1108
* Remove i18n keys from default flows by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1118
* Add i18n resolution support for the flow builder UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1120
* Add React SDK integration MCP tool by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1123
* Improve templated defaults in application MCP tools by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1127
* Improve thunder develop test coverage by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1135
* Update invite link for onboarding flow by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1143
* Change Internal Webauthn Wrapper Variables to Package Private and Replace Custom Mocks with Mockery by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1100
* Update default invite flow by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1146
* Update JWKS service to retrieve all certificates by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1137
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix setup.sh to run with bash by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1112
* Fix UserTypeResolver to return meta for the SDK UI rendering by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1106
* Fix SMS OTP executor to prompt mobile during registration by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1129
* Fix user schema export by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1102


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.18.0...v0.19.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.19.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.19.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.19.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/thunder-0.19.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.19.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/thunder-0.19.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.19.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/thunder-0.19.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.19.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/thunder-0.19.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.19.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/thunder-0.19.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.19.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.19.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.16.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.19.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.19.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.19.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.19.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.19.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.

bugs: Fix setup.sh to run with bash
Fix UserTypeResolver to return meta for the SDK UI rendering
Fix SMS OTP executor to prompt mobile during registration
Fix user schema export
features: Introduce initial MCP setup with application and flow tools
Add admin-initiated user registration flow support
Onboard application editing capabilities
Introduce User Invite feature
Add passkey executor support for flow execution
improvements: Add react-api-based-sample app to the release artifacts
Allow defining meta for TASK_EXECUTION nodes
Bump golang.org/x/crypto from 0.44.0 to 0.45.0 in /backend by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1108
Remove i18n keys from default flows
Add i18n resolution support for the flow builder UI
Add React SDK integration MCP tool
Improve templated defaults in application MCP tools
Improve thunder develop test coverage
Update invite link for onboarding flow
Change Internal Webauthn Wrapper Variables to Package Private and Replace Custom Mocks with Mockery
Update default invite flow
Update JWKS service to retrieve all certificates

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.18.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Introduce prompts to prompt nodes and improve input handling by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1071
### 🚀 Features
* Add Atomic API based Passkey Registration and Authentication Support by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/987
* Add transaction management infrastructure by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/976
### ✨ Improvements
* Modify prompt nodes to use prompts and improve input handling by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1074
* Improve server started log to indicate the startup time by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1064
* Refactor crypto services by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1066
* Move to `Thunder` branding by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1097
* Add Atomic API react sample app to thunder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1057
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix i18n message overriding precedence by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1040


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.17.0...v0.18.0

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)
