# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.17.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Rename flow graph id to flow id by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1020
* Add config to change min TLS version by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1038
* Refactor: change credential storage from array to Map by @KaveeshaPiumini in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/958
### 🚀 Features
* Support multiple algorithms for JWT signing by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1019
### ✨ Improvements
* Update flow docs by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1025
* Integrate the thunder-logger by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1027
* Add immutable config support for I18n by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1007
* Update obtain Admin token documentation by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1037
* Improve logic in flow-builder UI and remove SCSS styling by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1024
* Introduce unit tests to the flow-builder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1041
* Improve test coverage in develop app by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1048
* Bump golang.org/x/net from 0.19.0 to 0.38.0 in /backend by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1050
* Bump google.golang.org/protobuf from 1.32.0 to 1.33.0 in /backend by @dependabot[bot] in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1051
* Update dockerfile UID&GID to 10001 by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1043
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Remove outdated config from helm deployment yaml by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1032
* Fix duplicate SMS OTP issue in the assurance authenticator list by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1029
* Fix wildcard export for user-schemas and ou by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1011


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.16.0...v0.17.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.17.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.17.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.17.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/thunder-0.17.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.17.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/thunder-0.17.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.17.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/thunder-0.17.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.17.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/thunder-0.17.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.17.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/thunder-0.17.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.17.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.17.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.16.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.17.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.17.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.17.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.17.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.17.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.16.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Improve flow definitions and execution API request/responses by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/920
* Define a generic START node for all flows  by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/922
* Add dedicated start/end nodes and remove inferred executors by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/929
* Integrate role permission with resource definition by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/944
* Remove legacy flow mgt and migrate default flow definitions by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/961
* Change Immutable resource directory by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/970
* Introduce an executor `mode` to allow two path execution by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/984

### 🚀 Features
* Implement login flow builder UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/918
* Add the new flow management implementation by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/934
* Add initial i18n framework by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/940

### ✨ Improvements
* Add meta to flow definitions and introduce verbose flag by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/926
* Refactor immutable resources code by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/928
* Allow basic auth executor to be used with any attribute by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/943
* Add layout information to the flow definition by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/946
* Introduce immutable handle to the flow management service by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/956
* Address Windows PowerShell Compatibility issues by @warnakulasuriya-fds-e23 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/951
* Introduce token issuance observability and refactor authentication events by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/947
* Add immutable config support for OU by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/953
* Use `component` metadata from `Flows` API  in Thunder Gate by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/966
* Composite store support for OU by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/960
* Reading deployment secrets from environment variables and file objects by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/937
* Add immutable resource support to flow graphs by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/975
* Allow provisioning executor to perform group and role assignment by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/979
* UX improvements to flow-builder UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/978
* Update flow UI definitions by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/988
* Add default prefix to bootstrap flow definitions by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/994
* Fix issues in flow export function by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/991
* Improvements to Flow Builder UI by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/995
* Integrate Branding API in Gate by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/990
* Update validation notification styling by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1001
* Update notification-senders options by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1000
* Add autolayout for flows without positioning data by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1002
* Add comprehensive guide for observability with analytics dashboard by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/999
* Add support for showing React SDK integration guides by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1004
* Add glob-pattern-based public path matching by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/981
* Improve layout of the flow builder by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1006
* Update default flow input types by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1010

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Update branding resolve API CORS by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/993
* Fix vanilla sample application by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1005
* Fix system token generation in readme to match flow execution changes by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1017
* Remove auth_flow_graph_id for the Client Credential App by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/1018


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.15.0...v0.16.0

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.16.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.16.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.16.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.16.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.16.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.


#### Obtain System API Token

To access the system APIs of Thunder, you need a token with system permissions. Follow the steps below to obtain a system API token.

1. Run the following command, replacing `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION"}'
```
2. Extract the `flowId` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"INCOMPLETE", ...}
```

3. Run the following command, replacing `<flow_id>` with the `flowId` value you extracted above.
```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"flowId":"<flow_id>", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"},"action": "action_001"}'
```

4. Obtain the system API token by extracting the `assertion` value from the response.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Application creation is secured functionality, so you first need to obtain a system API token as mentioned in the "Obtain System API Token" section above.

   Run the following command, replacing `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.

releases.name: Thunder v0.16.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.15.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ✨ Improvements
* Move AuthenticationContext from context to security by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/912
* Buffer encode HTTP responses before sending headers by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/921
* Add `application_template` to application api by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/924
* Remove OAuth jargon from Application Onboarding by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/914
* Add dynamic token issuer resolution by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/917
* Derive permission for Resources and Actions by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/909
* Add refresh token expiry time config by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/916
* Add fsGroup and runAsGroup support by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/933
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix db type issue and add Query method to Tx by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/936


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.14.0...v0.15.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.15.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.15.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.15.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/thunder-0.15.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.15.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/thunder-0.15.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.15.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/thunder-0.15.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.15.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/thunder-0.15.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.15.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/thunder-0.15.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.15.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.15.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.15.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.15.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.15.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.15.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.15.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.15.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

To try out the Client Credentials flow, you first need to obtain a token to access the System APIs of Thunder. Follow these steps:

Replace `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"}}'
```

The response will contain an `assertion` field.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application. Replace `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.14.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Add multi deployment support for data layer by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/836
* Improvement to the observability package by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/717
* Remove support for scripts in bootstrap/custom by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/809
* Update public url and add support to override app configs using helm by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/874
* Add PKCE validation for public clients by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/863
* Add support for indexed user attributes by @ThumulaPerera in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/892
* Improve IDP property/ error handling in auth services by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/902
* Support password hashing with customizable params by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/785
### 🚀 Features
* Add Resource API definition and Implementation by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/666
### ✨ Improvements
* Add WriteSuccessResponse/WriteErrorResponse helpers by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/845
* Support reading port from deployment.yaml in build script by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/835
* Add persistence layer for Sqlite databases by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/880
* Add http server support for helm chart by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/883
* Bump react, @wso2/oxygen-ui and @asgardeo/react versions to latest by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/885
* Remove depricated nginx annotations by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/888
* Add branding resolve implementation by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/881
* Update ingress and bootstrap configurations by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/889
* Support immutable configuraitons for Identity Providers by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/803
* Add authorization request store by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/873
* Uses spans and traces properly with OpenTelemetry by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/877
* Bump SDKs by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/895
* Add config for auth code expiry time by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/899
* Improve IDP property validations/ handling default properties by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/900
* Add immutable config support for notification senders and user schemas by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/884
* Comprehensive cleanup to use WriteSuccessResponse and WriteErrorResponse by @JeethJJ in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/878

## New Contributors
* @JeethJJ made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/845

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.13.0...v0.14.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.14.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.14.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.14.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/thunder-0.14.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.14.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/thunder-0.14.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.14.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/thunder-0.14.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.14.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/thunder-0.14.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.14.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/thunder-0.14.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.14.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.14.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker Compose

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker Compose.

1. **Download the Docker Compose file**

    Download the `docker-compose.yml` file using the following command:

    ```bash
    curl -o docker-compose.yml https://raw.githubusercontent.com/asgardeo/thunder/v0.14.0/install/quick-start/docker-compose.yml
    ```

2. **Start Thunder**

    Run the following command in the directory where you downloaded the `docker-compose.yml` file:

    ```bash
    docker compose up
    ```

    This will automatically:
    - Initialize the database
    - Run the setup process
    - Start the Thunder server

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>` in the setup logs.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.14.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.14.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.14.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.14.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.14.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

To try out the Client Credentials flow, you first need to obtain a token to access the System APIs of Thunder. Follow these steps:

Replace `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"}}'
```

The response will contain an `assertion` field.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application. Replace `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

releases.name: Thunder v0.14.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.13.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Introduce datasource for user data by @warnakulasuriya-fds-e23 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/677
* Improvements to the authorization code flow by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/781
### 🚀 Features
* Add support to exchange auth assertions to access tokens by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/718
* Add support for basic conditional node execution by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/849
* Introduce user self service API by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/826
### ✨ Improvements
* Move the immutable resources configs to default.json by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/796
* Refactor idp/ cert packages by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/798
* Improve application onboarding flow in Develop app by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/808
* Allow provisioning email address for federated users by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/823
* Add React SDK based application to samples by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/824
* [Develop] Enable username/password login by default by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/825
* Add OAuth settings to application onboarding by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/830
* Bump @asgardeo/react version to 0.6.8 by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/831
* Improve global styling + bump oxygen-ui version by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/829
* Add support for user provisioning in external IDP OAuth/OIDC authentication flows by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/837
* Remove intermediate error message during the social login redirect by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/840
* Remove array and object type property tests from CreateUserTypePage by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/842
* Remove sign-up link from sign-in page if self-registration is disabled by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/843
* Fix divider rendering in gate app by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/850
* Improve existing user validations for the  federated auth executors by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/853
* Bump @asgardeo/react to version 0.6.10 by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/858
* Add react-sdk sample to release pipeline by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/828
* Add common config to indicate gate app path by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/839
* Bump @asgardeo/react to version 0.6.11 by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/860
* Improve client configs in bootstrap scripts by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/864
* Add proper display names to org creation page by updating to @asgardeo/react to version 0.6.12 by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/867
* Remove ou description from the ou executor default inputs by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/866
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix Error while decrypting IDP properties by @warnakulasuriya-fds-e23 in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/723
* Change Issuer as Refresh Token audience by @Thumimku in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/819
* Fix SQLite Database Locking Issues Under Concurrent Load by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/846
* Template http_only server config in helm charts by @mevan-karu in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/855
* Fix Develop app menu overflow by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/856

## New Contributors
* @warnakulasuriya-fds-e23 made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/723
* @Thumimku made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/819
* @mevan-karu made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/855

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.12.0...v0.13.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.13.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.13.0 release**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.13.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/thunder-0.13.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.13.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/thunder-0.13.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.13.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/thunder-0.13.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.13.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/thunder-0.13.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.13.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/thunder-0.13.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.13.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.13.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker.

1. **Pull the Docker image**

    ```bash
    docker pull ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.13.0
    ```

2. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    ```bash
        docker run -it --rm \
            ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.13.0 \
            ./setup.sh
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    > [!NOTE]
    > This will shut down the container after the setup is complete. You need to start the container again using the command in step 3. If you are using sqlite as the database, then you need to mount a volume to persist the database file and share it between the setup and server run containers.

3. **Run the container**

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.13.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to modify the server configurations, you can mount a custom `deployment.yaml` file. Create a `deployment.yaml` file in your working directory similar to the [deployment.yaml](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/blob/main/backend/cmd/server/repository/conf/deployment.yaml), and mount it as below:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.13.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to use custom configurations or certificates, you can mount them as follows:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.cert:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.cert \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.key:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.key \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.13.0
    ```

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

Thunder provides two sample applications to help you get started quickly:

- **React Vanilla Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating direct API integration without external SDKs. Supports Native Flow API or Standard OAuth/OIDC.
- **React SDK Sample** — Sample React application demonstrating SDK-based integration using `@asgardeo/react` for OAuth 2.0/OIDC authentication.

##### React Vanilla Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-vanilla-0.13.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set the `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product":

    ```json
    {
        "applicationID": "{your-application-id}"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration options including OAuth redirect-based login.

##### React SDK Sample

1. **Download the sample**

   | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
   |-------|-------------|-------------|
   | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
   | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-macos-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-linux-x64.zip) |
   | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
   | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.13.0/sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory**

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-react-sdk-0.13.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the sample**

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to access the sample app.

    > 📖 Refer to the `README.md` inside the extracted sample app for detailed configuration and troubleshooting.

##### Self Register and Login (React Vanilla Sample)

The React Vanilla sample supports user self-registration and login:

1. Open [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click **"Sign up"** to register a new user.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.13.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

2. After registration, use the same credentials to **"Sign In"**.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.13.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. Upon successful login, you'll see the home page with your access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

To try out the Client Credentials flow, you first need to obtain a token to access the System APIs of Thunder. Follow these steps:

Replace `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"}}'
```

The response will contain an `assertion` field.
```json
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application. Replace `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
