releases.name: Thunder v0.13.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡

### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.12.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Use `idpId` in flow graphs instead of `idpName` by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/646
* [Refactoring 1] Improve flow engine and executors by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/685
* Securing Thunder APIs by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/708
* Improve application token resolve logic by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/751
* Improve the Thunder bootstrap experience by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/744
* Improve user schema to indicate which ou these users are getting created and whether they support self registration by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/756
* Change `Gate` app base path (`/signin` -> `/gate`) & Add `SignUp` support by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/762
* Add user type resolver to dynamically resolve userType and ouId in registration flows by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/759
* Enforce validation of the ou id of the users by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/774
### 🚀 Features
* Add http request executor by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/743
* Introduce branding support for applications by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/737
* Introduce Application Onboarding & Listing by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/752
* Add OIDC userinfo endpoint support by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/711
### ✨ Improvements
* UX improvements to the user creation flow by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/661
* Refactor authentication services to use di pattern by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/673
* Improvements to the observability configs by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/667
* Add i18n UI package to handle translations in apps by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/663
* Refactor message notification package by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/688
* Add collapsible sidebar for thunder-develop app by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/691
* Refactor OAuth and introduce token service by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/671
* Improve apps visual appearance by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/668
* Add OpenChoreo deployment artifacts by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/707
* [Refactoring 2] Improve flow services and executors by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/724
* Refactor crypto package and add sign verify methods by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/676
* Automate thunder helm chart release by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/720
* Add OAuth client authentication middleware by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/706
* Refactor API hooks in thunder-develop app to use Asgardeo's HTTP client by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/730
* Allow storing flexible node properties by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/736
* Add applicationld in the gate app redirection by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/740
* Update sidepanel styling and icons with test coverage improvements by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/741
* Add allowed user types to application by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/738
* Add ou details to the auth assertion by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/746
* Remove local ui packages and point to oxygen-ui by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/734
* Expose `logo_url` from Application Listing API by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/742
* Update healthcheck path for security check by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/755
* Add immutable config export support for applications by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/719
* Improvement to start and setup scripts by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/757
* Introduce service method to get user schema by name by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/761
* Enforce OU validation in the user schema by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/763
* Add error path as public by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/765
* Enhance `Gate` app's `SignUp` component to support `SELECT` inputs by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/767
* Improve user-schema UIs to include OU ID & allowSelfRegistration details by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/769
* Handle make run to setup thunder properly and fix middleware execution order by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/770
* Add userType and ou details to the oauth tokens by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/773
* Disable application view button by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/775
* Refactor db provider to have dedicated db client methods by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/778
* Improve user type UIs to indicate OU name instead of OU ID by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/779
* Improve docs to include token header in the system API calls by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/782
* Improve apps UI styling by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/788
* Improve ou handle validation to restrict forward slash by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/791
* FIx some UI sizing issues by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/792
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix incorrect `password` field type in Gate App by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/727
* Fix setup scripts to include ou in user schema creation by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/766
* Fix redirect based login in the sample app by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/771
* Fix create user ui to include correct ou by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/776
* Improve setup process to create resources required to the Quickstart guide by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/777

## New Contributors
* @KaveeshaPiumini made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/632

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.11.0...v0.12.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.12.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.12.0 release**

    | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
    |-------|-------------|-------------|
    | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.12.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/thunder-0.12.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
    | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.12.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/thunder-0.12.0-macos-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.12.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/thunder-0.12.0-linux-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.12.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/thunder-0.12.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
    | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.12.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/thunder-0.12.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.12.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.12.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./setup.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\setup.ps1
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Start the product**

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    ./start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

    The product will start on `https://localhost:8090`.

#### Option 2: Run with Docker

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker.

1. **Pull the Docker image**

    ```bash
    docker pull ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.12.0
    ```

2. **Setup the product**

    You need to setup the server with the initial configurations and data before starting the server for the first time.

    ```bash
        docker run -it --rm \
            ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.12.0 \
            ./setup.sh
    ```

    **Note the id of the sample app indicated with the log line `[INFO] Sample App ID: <id>`.** You'll need it for the sample app configuration.

    > [!NOTE]
    > This will shut down the container after the setup is complete. You need to start the container again using the command in step 3. If you are using sqlite as the database, then you need to mount a volume to persist the database file and share it between the setup and server run containers.

3. **Run the container**

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.12.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to modify the server configurations, you can mount a custom `deployment.yaml` file. Create a `deployment.yaml` file in your working directory similar to the [deployment.yaml](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/blob/main/backend/cmd/server/repository/conf/deployment.yaml), and mount it as below:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.12.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to use custom configurations or certificates, you can mount them as follows:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.cert:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.cert \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.key:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.key \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.12.0
    ```

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

Follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

To quickly get started with Thunder, you can use the sample app provided with the product. This guide demonstrates how to download and run the sample app, self register a user and try out login to the sample app.

##### Download and Run the Sample App

1. Download the sample app from the 0.12.0 release

    | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
    |-------|-------------|-------------|
    | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-0.12.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/sample-app-0.12.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
    | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-0.12.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/sample-app-0.12.0-macos-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-0.12.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/sample-app-0.12.0-linux-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-0.12.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/sample-app-0.12.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
    | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-0.12.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.12.0/sample-app-0.12.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-0.12.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-0.12.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Configure the sample app**

    Open `app/runtime.json` and set `applicationID` to the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

    For example, if the sample app ID is `d6df2ef9-88db-4ca9-9a23-d1577b2552c1`, `runtime.json` should look like:
    ```
    {
        "applicationID": "d6df2ef9-88db-4ca9-9a23-d1577b2552c1",
        "flowEndpoint": "https://localhost:8090/flow"
    }
    ```

4. **Start the sample app**

    ```bash
    sh start.sh
    ```

Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to see the sample app in action.

##### Self Register a User

To self register a user in the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click on the "Sign up" button.

2. Provide a username and password for the new user and click on the "Create Account" button.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.12.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

3. Fill in the additional user attributes such as first name, last name and email address. Click "Continue" to complete the registration.

    ![Self Registration Additional Details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.12.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-details.png)

4. After successful registration, you will be automatically logged in to the sample application.

##### Login to the Sample App

To log in to the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000).

2. Enter username and password you used during the self registration process and click on the "Sign In" button.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.12.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. If the login is successful, you will be redirected to the home page of the sample app with the access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

To try out the Client Credentials flow, you first need to obtain a token to access the System APIs of Thunder. Follow these steps:

Replace `<application_id>` with the sample app ID generated during "Setup the product."

```bash
curl -k -X POST 'https://localhost:8090/flow/execute' \
  -d '{"applicationId":"<application_id>","flowType":"AUTHENTICATION", "inputs":{"username":"admin","password":"admin", "requested_permissions":"system"}}'
```

The response will contain an `assertion` field.
```
{"flowId":"<flow_id>","flowStatus":"COMPLETE","data":{},"assertion":"<assertion>"}
```


The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application. Replace `<assertion>` with the assertion value obtained from the previous step.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <assertion>' \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡
### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

<!-- Release notes generated using configuration in .github/release.yml at v0.11.0 -->

## What's Changed
### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
* Update token endpoint auth method by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/560
* Initiate login flow from oauth before redirecting to gate by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/624
* Make user schema validation mandatory by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/637
### 🚀 Features
* Onboard React based `Gate` & `Develop` secured with `@asgardeo/react` SDK by @brionmario in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/584
* Implement user managment UI in thunder-develop application by @DonOmalVindula in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/597
* Add support for generating auth assurance levels by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/565
* Introduce DCR support by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/571
* Modify auth executors to generate and add auth assurance by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/580
* Add OAuth/OIDC discovery endpoints by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/613
* Add immutable configuration support for Applications by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/587
* Add ou creation executor and improve the provisioning flow by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/633
* Role management implementation by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/555
* Add token exchange support by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/634
* Add initial observability component by @sahandilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/589
### ✨ Improvements
* Refactor DI pattern of oauth package by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/520
* Refactor crypto and hash packages by @hwupathum in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/568
* Role API definition by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/552
* Add OAuth resource indicator support by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/604
* Refactor cert configuration initialization by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/598
* Add user type and ou to the flow assertion by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/621
* Refactor system cert service usage by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/623
* Remove application service provider by @rajithacharith in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/627
* RBAC support for flow engine and auth code flow by @senthalan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/641
* Improve UI theming by onboarding oxygen-ui dependency by @jeradrutnam in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/647
### 🐛 Bug Fixes
* Fix PostgreSQL query issues by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/551
* Update default container image tag by @ayeshajay in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/563
* Add certificate for JWT in HTTP mode by @thiva-k in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/588
* Fix google login with flow execution by @ThaminduDilshan in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/594
* Fix limit param validation in user schema listing by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/636
* Update user api spec to reflect latest changes by @darshanasbg in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/645

## New Contributors
* @iff-sal made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/506
* @ayeshajay made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/563

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/compare/v0.10.0...v0.11.0

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.11.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.11.0 release**

| OS | Architecture | Download Link |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.11.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/thunder-0.11.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
| macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.11.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/thunder-0.11.0-macos-x64.zip) |
| Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.11.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/thunder-0.11.0-linux-x64.zip) |
| Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.11.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/thunder-0.11.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
| Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.11.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/thunder-0.11.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.11.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.11.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the product**

    Start the product using the following command:

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    bash start.sh --setup
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1 --setup
    ```

#### Option 2: Run with Docker

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker.

1. **Pull the Docker image**

    ```bash
    docker pull ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.11.0
    ```

2. **Run the container**

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.11.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to modify the server configurations, you can mount a custom `deployment.yaml` file. Create a `deployment.yaml` file in your working directory similar to the [deployment.yaml](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/blob/main/backend/cmd/server/repository/conf/deployment.yaml), and mount it as below:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.11.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to use custom configurations or certificates, you can mount them as follows:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.cert:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.cert \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.key:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.key \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.11.0
    ```

### Try Out the Product

#### Try out the Developer Console

To get started quickly, you can set up initial data including an admin user and the Develop application using the `--setup` flag when starting Thunder:

**Linux/macOS:**

```bash
bash start.sh --setup
```

**Windows (PowerShell):**

```powershell
.\start.ps1 --setup
```

This will:

- Create a user schema
- Create an admin user with credentials: `admin` / `admin`
- Create the Develop application for accessing the admin console

You can also run the setup script manually at any time:

**Linux/macOS:**

```bash
bash scripts/setup_initial_data.sh
```

**Windows (PowerShell):**

```powershell
.\scripts\setup_initial_data.ps1
```

After the setup is complete, follow these steps to access the Developer Console:

1. Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:8090/develop](https://localhost:8090/develop).

2. Log in using the admin credentials created during the initial data setup (`admin` / `admin`).

#### Try Out with the Sample App

To quickly get started with Thunder, you can use the sample app provided with the product. This guide demonstrates how to download and run the sample app, self register a user and try out login to the sample app.

##### Download and Run the Sample App

1. Download the sample app from the 0.11.0 release

| OS | Architecture | Download Link |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-0.11.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/sample-app-0.11.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
| macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-0.11.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/sample-app-0.11.0-macos-x64.zip) |
| Linux | x64 | [sample-app-0.11.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/sample-app-0.11.0-linux-x64.zip) |
| Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-0.11.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/sample-app-0.11.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
| Windows | x64 | [sample-app-0.11.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.11.0/sample-app-0.11.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-0.11.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-0.11.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Create required application for sample app in Thunder**

    Before using the sample app, you need to create an application in Thunder:

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Sample App",
        "description": "Sample application for testing",
        "url": "https://localhost:3000",
        "logo_url": "https://localhost:3000/logo.png",
        "tos_uri": "https://localhost:3000/terms",
        "policy_uri": "https://localhost:3000/privacy",
        "contacts": ["admin@example.com", "support@example.com"],
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "registration_flow_graph_id": "registration_flow_config_basic",
        "is_registration_flow_enabled": true,
        "user_attributes": ["given_name","family_name","email","groups"],
        "inbound_auth_config": [{
            "type": "oauth2",
            "config": {
                "client_id": "sample_app_client",
                "client_secret": "sample_app_secret",
                "redirect_uris": ["https://localhost:3000"],
                "grant_types": ["authorization_code", "client_credentials"],
                "response_types": ["code"],
                "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                "pkce_required": false,
                "public_client": false,
                "scopes": ["openid", "profile", "email"],
                "token": {
                    "issuer": "thunder",
                    "access_token": {
                        "validity_period": 3600,
                        "user_attributes": ["given_name","family_name","email","groups"]
                    },
                    "id_token": {
                        "validity_period": 3600,
                        "user_attributes": ["given_name","family_name","email","groups"],
                        "scope_claims": {
                            "profile": ["name","given_name","family_name","picture"],
                            "email": ["email","email_verified"],
                            "phone": ["phone_number","phone_number_verified"],
                            "group": ["groups"]
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }]
    }'
    ```

    Note the `id` from the response - you'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Configure the sample app**

    Open the `runtime.json` file in the thunder-sample-app-0.11.0-<os>-<arch>/app directory and update the configurations:
    - `applicationID`: Use the application ID from step 3
    - `flowEndpoint`: The root endpoint for the flow execution API (default: `https://localhost:8090/flow`)

5. **Start the sample app**

    ```bash
    sh start.sh
    ```

Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to see the sample app in action.

##### Self Register a User

To self register a user in the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click on the "Sign up" button.

2. Provide a username and password for the new user and click on the "Create Account" button.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.11.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

3. Fill in the additional user attributes such as first name, last name and email address. Click "Continue" to complete the registration.

    ![Self Registration Additional Details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.11.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-details.png)

4. After successful registration, you will be automatically logged in to the sample application.

##### Login to the Sample App

To log in to the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000).

2. Enter username and password you used during the self registration process and click on the "Sign In" button.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.11.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. If the login is successful, you will be redirected to the home page of the sample app with the access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic",
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false,
                    "scopes": ["api:read", "api:write"]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

releases.name: Thunder v0.11.0

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡
### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

## 🔀 What's Changed

### ⚠️ Breaking Changes
- Remove users/authenticate API #515 by @ThaminduDilshan

### 🚀 Features
- Add user credentials authenticate API and service/ Refactoring #480 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Introduce initial ID token support #521 by @thiva-k
- Embed Gate app to Thunder #540 by @brionmario

### ✨ Improvements
- Use correct error when retrieving flowContext #465 by @rajithacharith
- Change CORS to middleware pattern #464 by @senthalan
- Refactor encrypted property handling #466 by @thiva-k
- Handle server graceful shutdown #471 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Initial refactor for dependency injection #474 by @senthalan
- Improve pagination links in user list endpoint #489 by @darshanasbg
- Refactoring user error constants #490 by @darshanasbg
- Refactor DI pattern of userschema package #483 by @senthalan
- Refactor DI pattern of ou package #485 by @senthalan
- Refactor DI pattern of group package #486 by @senthalan
- Reuse credential authn service in basic auth executor #495 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Refactor DI pattern of user package #487 by @senthalan
- Add assertion generation to the authn service and APIs #499 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Add validation for public clients #492 by @thiva-k
- Refactor flowexec and flowmgt service to use DI #498 by @rajithacharith
- Refactor deployment structure #491 by @drsamitha
- Refactor application service to use DI #519 by @rajithacharith
- Refactor cert service to use DI #525 by @rajithacharith
- Add support to list groups from user api #488 by @darshanasbg
- Add support to retrieve user groups in OAuth tokens #542 by @ThaminduDilshan

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Add default timeouts to notification sender clients #467 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Remove escape character from the logs #530 by @sahandilshan
- Fix id token storage for apps #545 by @ThaminduDilshan

## New Contributors
* @malinthaprasan made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/454
* @drsamitha made their first contribution in https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/491

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.10.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.10.0 release**

    | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
    |-------|-------------|-------------|
    | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.10.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/thunder-0.10.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
    | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.10.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/thunder-0.10.0-macos-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.10.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/thunder-0.10.0-linux-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.10.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/thunder-0.10.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
    | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.10.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/thunder-0.10.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.10.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.10.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the product**

    Start the product using the following command:

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    bash start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

#### Option 2: Run with Docker

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker.

1. **Pull the Docker image**

    ```bash
    docker pull ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.10.0
    ```

2. **Run the container**

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -p 9090:9090 \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.10.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to modify the server configurations, you can mount a custom `deployment.yaml` file. Create a `deployment.yaml` file in your working directory similar to the [deployment.yaml](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/blob/main/backend/cmd/server/repository/conf/deployment.yaml), and mount it as below:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -p 8090:9090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.10.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to use custom configurations or certificates, you can mount them as follows:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -p 8090:9090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.cert:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.cert \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.key:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.key \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.10.0
    ```

### Try Out the Product

#### Try Out with the Sample App

To quickly get started with Thunder, you can use the sample app provided with the product. This guide demonstrates how to download and run the sample app, self register a user and try out login to the sample app.

##### Download and Run the Sample App

1. Download the sample app from the 0.10.0 release

    | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
    |-------|-------------|-------------|
    | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-0.10.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/sample-app-0.10.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
    | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-0.10.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/sample-app-0.10.0-macos-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-0.10.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/sample-app-0.10.0-linux-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-0.10.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/sample-app-0.10.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
    | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-0.10.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.10.0/sample-app-0.10.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-0.10.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-0.10.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Create required application for sample app in Thunder**

    Before using the sample app, you need to create an application in Thunder:

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Sample App",
        "description": "Sample application for testing",
        "url": "https://localhost:3000",
        "logo_url": "https://localhost:3000/logo.png",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "registration_flow_graph_id": "registration_flow_config_basic",
        "is_registration_flow_enabled": true,
        "user_attributes": ["given_name","family_name","email","groups"],
        "inbound_auth_config": [{
            "type": "oauth2",
            "config": {
                "client_id": "sample_app_client",
                "client_secret": "sample_app_secret",
                "redirect_uris": ["https://localhost:3000"],
                "grant_types": ["authorization_code", "client_credentials"],
                "response_types": ["code"],
                "token_endpoint_auth_methods": ["client_secret_basic", "client_secret_post"],
                "pkce_required": false,
                "public_client": false,
                "token": {
                    "issuer": "thunder",
                    "access_token": {
                        "validity_period": 3600,
                        "user_attributes": ["given_name","family_name","email","groups"]
                    },
                    "id_token": {
                        "validity_period": 3600,
                        "user_attributes": ["given_name","family_name","email","groups"],
                        "scope_claims": {
                            "profile": ["name","given_name","family_name","picture"],
                            "email": ["email","email_verified"],
                            "phone": ["phone_number","phone_number_verified"],
                            "group": ["groups"]
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }]
    }'
    ```

    Note the `id` from the response - you'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Configure the sample app**

    Open the `runtime.json` file in the thunder-sample-app-<version>-<os>-<arch>/app directory and update the configurations:
    - `applicationID`: Use the application ID from step 3
    - `flowEndpoint`: The root endpoint for the flow execution API (default: `https://localhost:8090/flow`)

5. **Start the sample app**

    ```bash
    sh start.sh
    ```

Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to see the sample app in action.

##### Self Register a User

To self register a user in the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click on the "Sign up" button.

2. Provide a username and password for the new user and click on the "Create Account" button.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.10.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

3. Fill in the additional user attributes such as first name, last name and email address. Click "Continue" to complete the registration.

    ![Self Registration Additional Details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.10.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-details.png)

4. After successful registration, you will be automatically logged in to the sample application.

##### Login to the Sample App

To log in to the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000).

2. Enter username and password you used during the self registration process and click on the "Sign In" button.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.10.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. If the login is successful, you will be redirected to the home page of the sample app with the access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_methods": [
                        "client_secret_basic",
                        "client_secret_post"
                    ],
                    "pkce_required": false,
                    "public_client": false
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

releases.body:
# WSO2 Thunder ⚡
### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

## 🔀 What's Changed

### 🚀 Features
- Add OTP send and verify API endpoints #416 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Introduce IDP type and add property validations #434 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Add federated IDP authentication API and service implementation #446 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Add SMS OTP authentication API and service implementation #450 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Introduce PKCE support #436 by @thiva-k

### ✨ Improvements
- Modify sms auth executor to reuse OTP service #417 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Add encryption to secret properties in identity providers and notification senders #420 by @thiva-k
- Add OAuth and Github auth core services #425 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Add OIDC and Google auth core services #428 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Refactor the IDP package/ service #433 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Refactor hashing logic to use new util methods #350 by @hwupathum
- Improve secret properties handling #447 by @thiva-k
- Add methods to verify jwt tokens without passing public key #451 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Improve OAuth app client ID and secret generation to align with OAuth 2.1 specifications with type-safe API #407 by @copilot

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fix issue with encrypted properties in new auth services #430 by @ThaminduDilshan
- Update hash usage #437 by @thiva-k
- Add jwt verify methods and fix usages #440 by @ThaminduDilshan

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download the 0.9.0 release of WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution from the 0.9.0 release**

    | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
    |-------|-------------|-------------|
    | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [thunder-0.9.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/thunder-0.9.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
    | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [thunder-0.9.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/thunder-0.9.0-macos-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | x64 | [thunder-0.9.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/thunder-0.9.0-linux-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | ARM64 | [thunder-0.9.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/thunder-0.9.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
    | Windows | x64 | [thunder-0.9.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/thunder-0.9.0-win-x64.zip) |


2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-0.9.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-0.9.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the product**

    Start the product using the following command:

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    bash start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```powershell
    .\start.ps1
    ```

#### Option 2: Run with Docker

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker.

1. **Pull the Docker image**

    ```bash
    docker pull ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.9.0
    ```

2. **Run the container**

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.9.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to modify the server configurations, you can mount a custom `deployment.yaml` file. Create a `deployment.yaml` file in your working directory similar to the [deployment.yaml](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/blob/main/backend/cmd/server/repository/conf/deployment.yaml), and mount it as below:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.9.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to use custom configurations or certificates, you can mount them as follows:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.cert:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.cert \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.key:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.key \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.9.0
    ```

### Try Out the Product

#### Try Out with the Sample App

To quickly get started with Thunder, you can use the sample app provided with the product. This guide demonstrates how to download and run the sample app, self register a user and try out login to the sample app.

##### Download and Run the Sample App

1. Download the sample app from the 0.9.0 release

    | OS | Architecture | Download Link |
    |-------|-------------|-------------|
    | macOS | ARM64 (Apple Silicon) | [sample-app-0.9.0-macos-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/sample-app-0.9.0-macos-arm64.zip) |
    | macOS | x64 (Intel) | [sample-app-0.9.0-macos-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/sample-app-0.9.0-macos-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | x64 | [sample-app-0.9.0-linux-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/sample-app-0.9.0-linux-x64.zip) |
    | Linux | ARM64 | [sample-app-0.9.0-linux-arm64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/sample-app-0.9.0-linux-arm64.zip) |
    | Windows | x64 | [sample-app-0.9.0-win-x64.zip](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/download/v0.9.0/sample-app-0.9.0-win-x64.zip) |

2. Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory

    ```bash
    unzip sample-app-0.9.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd sample-app-0.9.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Create required application for sample app in Thunder**

    Before using the sample app, you need to create an application in Thunder:

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Sample App",
        "description": "Sample application for testing",
        "url": "https://localhost:3000",
        "logo_url": "https://localhost:3000/logo.png",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "registration_flow_graph_id": "registration_flow_config_basic",
        "is_registration_flow_enabled": true,
        "inbound_auth_config": [{
            "type": "oauth2",
            "config": {
                "client_id": "sample_app_client",
                "client_secret": "sample_app_secret",
                "redirect_uris": ["https://localhost:3000"],
                "grant_types": ["authorization_code", "client_credentials"],
                "response_types": ["code"],
                "token_endpoint_auth_methods": ["client_secret_basic", "client_secret_post"],
                "pkce_required": true
            }
        }]
    }'
    ```

    Note the `id` from the response - you'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Configure the sample app**

    Open the `runtime.json` file in the thunder-sample-app-0.9.0-<os>-<arch>/app directory and update the configurations:
    - `applicationID`: Use the application ID from step 3
    - `flowEndpoint`: The root endpoint for the flow execution API (default: `https://localhost:8090/flow`)

5. **Start the sample app**

    ```bash
    sh start.sh
    ```

Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to see the sample app in action.

##### Self Register a User

To self register a user in the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click on the "Sign up" button.

2. Provide a username and password for the new user and click on the "Create Account" button.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.9.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

3. Fill in the additional user attributes such as first name, last name and email address. Click "Continue" to complete the registration.

    ![Self Registration Additional Details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.9.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-details.png)

4. After successful registration, you will be automatically logged in to the sample application.

##### Login to the Sample App

To log in to the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000).

2. Enter username and password you used during the self registration process and click on the "Sign In" button.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.9.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. If the login is successful, you will be redirected to the home page of the sample app with the access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_methods": [
                        "client_secret_basic",
                        "client_secret_post"
                    ],
                    "pkce_required": true
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
    ```

# WSO2 Thunder ⚡
### Identity Management Suite

Thunder is a modern, open-source identity management service designed for teams building secure, customizable authentication experiences across applications, services, and AI agents. It enables developers to design and orchestrate login, registration, and recovery flows using a flexible identity flow designer.

Designed for extensibility, scalability, and seamless containerized deployment, Thunder integrates naturally with microservices and DevOps environments—serving as the core identity layer for your cloud platform.

## 🔀 What's Changed

### 🚀 Features
- Add basic filter support for user listing ([#369](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/369)) by [@darshanasbg](https://github.com/darshanasbg)
- Add user-schema API ([#355](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/355)) by [@darshanasbg](https://github.com/darshanasbg)
- Improve default config handling with a default.json file ([#351](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/351)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Introduce token configs to application API and services ([#347](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/347)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Add OAuth 2.0 token introspection endpoint ([#336](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/336)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Introduce the initial crypto service ([#85](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/85)) by [@Osara-B](https://github.com/Osara-B)

### ✨ Improvements
- Update Docker image to add user ID and group ID ([#371](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/371)) by [@Lakshan-Banneheke](https://github.com/Lakshan-Banneheke)
- Introduce user schema enforcement to the user operations ([#363](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/363)) by [@darshanasbg](https://github.com/darshanasbg)
- Improve auth assertion to use application token configurations ([#354](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/354)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Introduce server-level CORS configs ([#356](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/356)) by [@thiva-k](https://github.com/thiva-k)
- Improve standard grant JWT tokens to use application token configurations ([#353](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/353)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Improve caching in 'allowed origins' ([#357](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/357)) by [@darshanasbg](https://github.com/darshanasbg)
- Refactor the jwt service and util into system/jwt package ([#348](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/348)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Improve OAuth app storing to accept a JSON column for additional configs ([#344](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/344)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Improve application API to accept inbound_auth_configs seperately ([#338](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/338)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)
- Fix gate app build errors ([#304](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/304)) by [@PasinduRavimal](https://github.com/PasinduRavimal)
- Remove userprodiver & groupprovider ([#331](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/331)) by [@darshanasbg](https://github.com/darshanasbg)
- Refactor OAuth2 package with injectable code ([#328](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/328)) by [@ThaminduDilshan](https://github.com/ThaminduDilshan)

### 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fix sample app's runtime.json resolution ([#374](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/374)) by [@thiva-k](https://github.com/thiva-k)
- Fix improper error response for token request ([#340](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/pull/340)) by [@thiva-k](https://github.com/thiva-k)

## ⚡ Quickstart

This Quickstart guide will help you get started with WSO2 Thunder quickly. It walks you through downloading and running the product, trying out the sample app, and exploring registering a user, logging in, and using the Client Credentials flow.

### Download and Run WSO2 Thunder

You can run WSO2 Thunder either by downloading the release artifact or using the official Docker image.

#### Option 1: Run from Release Artifact

Follow these steps to download WSO2 Thunder and run it locally.

1. **Download the distribution**

    Download `thunder-v0.7.0-<os>-<arch>.zip` from the [this release](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/v0.7.0) for your operating system and architecture.

    For example, if you are using a MacOS machine with a Apple Silicon (ARM64) processor, you would download `thunder-v0.7.0-macos-arm64.zip`.

2. **Unzip the product**

    Unzip the downloaded file using the following command:

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-v0.7.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    ```

    Navigate to the unzipped directory:

    ```bash
    cd thunder-v0.7.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Start the product**

    Start the product using the following command:

    If you are using a Linux or macOS machine:

    ```bash
    bash start.sh
    ```

    If you are using a Windows machine:

    ```bash
    start.bat
    ```

#### Option 2: Run with Docker

Follow these steps to run WSO2 Thunder using Docker.

1. **Pull the Docker image**

    ```bash
    docker pull ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.7.0
    ```

2. **Run the container**

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.7.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to modify the server configurations, you can mount a custom `deployment.yaml` file. Create a `deployment.yaml` file in your working directory similar to the [deployment.yaml](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/blob/main/backend/cmd/server/repository/conf/deployment.yaml), and mount it as below:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.7.0
    ```

    Optionally if you want to use custom configurations or certificates, you can mount them as follows:

    ```bash
    docker run --rm \
      -p 8090:8090 \
      -v $(pwd)/deployment.yaml:/opt/thunder/repository/conf/deployment.yaml \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.cert:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.cert \
      -v $(pwd)/certs/server.key:/opt/thunder/repository/resources/security/server.key \
      ghcr.io/asgardeo/thunder:0.7.0
    ```

### Try Out the Product

#### Try Out with the Sample App

To quickly get started with Thunder, you can use the sample app provided with the product. This guide demonstrates how to download and run the sample app, self register a user and try out login to the sample app.

##### Download and Run the Sample App

1. Download the sample app

    Download `thunder-sample-app-0.7.0-<os>-<arch>.zip` from the [this release](https://github.com/asgardeo/thunder/releases/v0.7.0) for your operating system and architecture.

2. Unzip and navigate to the sample app directory

    ```bash
    unzip thunder-sample-app-0.7.0-<os>-<arch>.zip
    cd thunder-sample-app-0.7.0-<os>-<arch>/
    ```

3. **Create required entities for the sample app**

    Before using the sample app, you need to create the required entities. Follow these steps:

    a. **Create a Local Identity Provider** (required for basic authentication):

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/identity-providers \
    -d '{
        "name": "Local",
        "description": "Local Identity Provider for basic authentication"
    }'
    ```

    b. **Create an Application**:

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Sample App",
        "description": "Sample application for testing",
        "url": "https://localhost:3000",
        "logo_url": "https://localhost:3000/logo.png",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "registration_flow_graph_id": "registration_flow_config_basic",
        "is_registration_flow_enabled": true,
        "inbound_auth_config": [{
            "type": "oauth2",
            "config": {
                "client_id": "sample_app_client",
                "client_secret": "sample_app_secret",
                "redirect_uris": ["https://localhost:3000"],
                "grant_types": ["authorization_code", "client_credentials"],
                "response_types": ["code"],
                "token_endpoint_auth_methods": ["client_secret_basic", "client_secret_post"]
            }
        }]
    }'
    ```

    Note the `id` from the response - you'll need it for the sample app configuration.

4. **Configure the sample app**

    Open the `runtime.json` file in the thunder-sample-app-0.7.0-<os>-<arch>/app directory and update the configurations:
    - `applicationID`: Use the application ID from step 3b
    - `flowEndpoint`: The root endpoint for the flow execution API (default: `https://localhost:8090/flow`)

5. **Start the sample app**

    ```bash
    sh start.sh
    ```

Open your browser and navigate to [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) to see the sample app in action.

##### Self Register a User

To self register a user in the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000) and click on the "Sign up" button.

2. Provide a username and password for the new user and click on the "Create Account" button.

    ![Self Registration Username Password](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.7.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-basic.png)

3. Fill in the additional user attributes such as first name, last name and email address. Click "Continue" to complete the registration.

    ![Self Registration Additional Details](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.7.0/resources/images/sample-app-self-registration-details.png)

4. After successful registration, you will be automatically logged in to the sample application.

##### Login to the Sample App

To log in to the sample app, follow these steps:

1. Open the sample app in your browser at [https://localhost:3000](https://localhost:3000).

2. Enter username and password you used during the self registration process and click on the "Sign In" button.

    ![Login to Sample App](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/thunder-id/thunderid/v0.7.0/resources/images/sample-app-login.png)

3. If the login is successful, you will be redirected to the home page of the sample app with the access token.

#### Try Out Client Credentials Flow

The Client Credentials flow is used to obtain an access token for machine-to-machine communication. This flow does not require user interaction and is typically used for server-to-server communication.

To try out the Client Credentials flow, follow these steps:

1. Create a Client Application

   Create a client application in the system to use for the Client Credentials flow. You can use the following cURL command to create a new application.

    ```bash
    curl -kL -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'Accept: application/json' https://localhost:8090/applications \
    -d '{
        "name": "Test Sample App",
        "description": "Initial testing App",
        "auth_flow_graph_id": "auth_flow_config_basic",
        "inbound_auth_config": [
            {
                "type": "oauth2",
                "config": {
                    "client_id": "<client_id>",
                    "client_secret": "<client_secret>",
                    "redirect_uris": [
                        "https://localhost:3000"
                    ],
                    "grant_types": [
                        "client_credentials"
                    ],
                    "token_endpoint_auth_methods": [
                        "client_secret_basic",
                        "client_secret_post"
                    ]
                }
            }
        ]
    }'
    ```

2. Obtain an Access Token

   Use the following cURL command to obtain an access token using the Client Credentials flow. Make sure to replace the `<client_id>` and `<client_secret>` with the values you used when creating the client application.

    ```bash
    curl -k -X POST https://localhost:8090/oauth2/token \
      -d 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
      -u '<client_id>:<client_secret>'
    ```

## License

Licenses this source under the Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE](LICENSE)), You may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
(c) Copyright 2025 WSO2 LLC.
